notes: Agronomy playbook for practical, field-level decision support. Use with local labels and extension guidance.

actions: Maintain weed-free rows during first 3 to 4 weeks.
Avoid waterlogging in seedling stage; improve drainage if needed.
If chlorosis appears early, confirm nitrogen and sulfur availability with a quick soil check.
cautions: Do not over-apply nitrogen in one shot on sandy soils.
Avoid herbicide stress under heat and moisture deficit.

actions: Split nitrogen application across growth windows where rainfall risk is high.
Prioritize irrigation consistency to reduce stress before tasseling.
Scout for foliar disease weekly in humid conditions.
cautions: Water stress near tasseling can cause major yield loss.
Unbalanced nitrogen and potassium can increase lodging risk.

actions: Keep water depth stable based on local rice management practice.
Use split nutrient timing to match tillering and panicle initiation.
Monitor for blast risk when humidity is persistently high.
cautions: Excess nitrogen late can increase disease pressure and lodging.
Poor drainage around field edges can drive patchy stress.

actions: Protect moisture during flowering and pod set windows.
Prioritize pest scouting for pod feeders and defoliators.
Keep field records of stress windows for next-season adjustments.
cautions: Short drought during pod set can reduce final yield strongly.
Avoid unnecessary sprays without threshold-based scouting.

symptom_to_likely_causes.symptom: leaf spots expanding during humid weather
first_actions: remove heavily infected debris where practical
consider registered fungicide per label and threshold

symptom_to_likely_causes.symptom: wilting at midday with recovery at evening
first_actions: reduce surface evaporation with residue or mulch
break compaction in next prep cycle

checks: inspect roots and stems in affected zones
first_actions: zone-based correction instead of whole-field uniform treatment

irrigation_rules_of_thumb.guidance: Use shorter, more frequent irrigation cycles to reduce stress spikes.
Delay additional irrigation and prioritize drainage checks before the next watering decision.
Avoid moisture gaps in reproductive windows; these stages are highly yield-sensitive.

question_patterns.recommendation_style: Start with a decision, state evidence, then provide a 3-step action plan and one safety caution.

safety_and_compliance: Always follow product labels and local registration rules.
Use threshold-based pest and disease decisions where possible.
Do not mix products unless label compatibility is confirmed.
Escalate to local agronomist or extension service for severe or fast-spreading symptoms.
